objects.name: Odyssey Stealer macOS Investigation - GHOST-2026-0427-ODYSSEY
objects.description: Investigation of Odyssey Stealer macOS information stealer, a MaaS platform targeting cryptocurrency wallets and browser credentials. Active C2 panels confirmed at charge0x[.]at and something0x[.]at.

objects.name: Odyssey Stealer
objects.description: macOS information stealer operating as Malware-as-a-Service (MaaS). Successor to Poseidon Stealer and descendant of AMOS (Atomic macOS Stealer). Targets 203 browser wallet extensions and 18 desktop wallet applications. Deploys persistent SOCKS5 proxy and supports arbitrary shell execution. Distributed via ClickFix social engineering.

objects.name: Rodrigo4
objects.description: Developer of Odyssey Stealer, previously created AMOS (Atomic macOS Stealer) and Poseidon Stealer. Active on XSS underground forum. Operates MaaS at $3,000/month with up to 15 affiliates.

objects.name: Odyssey Stealer Sample MD5
objects.description: MD5 hash of Odyssey Stealer sample reported by @suyog41 on 2026-04-27

objects.name: Odyssey Stealer Active C2 Domains
objects.description: Active C2 admin panel domains for Odyssey Stealer confirmed live 2026-04-27

objects.name: Odyssey Stealer Active C2 IPs
objects.description: Active C2 IP addresses hosting Odyssey Stealer admin panels

objects.name: Odyssey Stealer SOCKS5 Proxy Hash
objects.description: SHA256 of SOCKS5 proxy binary deployed by Odyssey Stealer on victim machines

objects.name: Odyssey Stealer Distribution Domains
objects.description: Domains used to distribute Odyssey Stealer via ClickFix social engineering
objects.pattern: [domain-name:value = 'bonoud.com' OR domain-name:value = 'allteching.xyz' OR domain-name:value = 'teamsonsoft.com' OR domain-name:value = 'bombauthority.website' OR domain-name:value = 'tradingviewen.com' OR domain-name:value = 'homebrewonline.org' OR domain-name:value = 'homebrewupdate.org']

objects.name: Odyssey Stealer Historical C2 IPs
objects.description: Historical C2 IP addresses associated with Odyssey Stealer infrastructure
objects.pattern: [ipv4-addr:value = '45.146.130.131' OR ipv4-addr:value = '185.93.89.62' OR ipv4-addr:value = '5.199.166.102' OR ipv4-addr:value = '77.90.185.24' OR ipv4-addr:value = '185.11.61.84' OR ipv4-addr:value = '45.135.232.33' OR ipv4-addr:value = '83.222.190.214' OR ipv4-addr:value = '93.152.230.79' OR ipv4-addr:value = '195.82.147.38' OR ipv4-addr:value = '38.244.158.56']

objects.name: Odyssey Stealer Host Artifacts
objects.description: File paths created by Odyssey Stealer on victim machines for persistence and data staging
objects.pattern: [file:name = '.botid' OR file:name = '.chost' OR file:name = '.pwd' OR file:parent_directory_ref.path = '/Library/LaunchDaemons' AND file:name MATCHES 'com\\.\\d{5}\\.plist']

objects.name: ClickFix Social Engineering
objects.description: Odyssey Stealer uses fake CAPTCHA pages on typosquatted domains that detect macOS and prompt users to paste base64-encoded curl|bash commands in Terminal. The decoded command downloads an obfuscated AppleScript payload.

objects.name: Odyssey Stealer MaaS Infrastructure
objects.description: Affiliate-based MaaS platform with centralized admin panels, distributed C2 servers across Netherlands, Romania, Kazakhstan, Russia, Singapore, and Lithuania. Panels are React SPAs served over HTTP only. Infrastructure uses AS208137 (FPS12), AS213790 (LimitedNetwork), AS57523 (Changway - bulletproof) among others.
